seoTitle: How to Add Images Into Power BI Tables, Cards & Visuals
metaDescription: See how to display images inside Power BI tables, cards, and other visuals to make reports more engaging and easier to scan at a glance.
intro: Text and numbers only get you so far in a report — sometimes a product photo, headshot, or icon communicates faster than any label. This guide covers how to bring images into Power BI visuals like tables and cards so your data has visual context. It's aimed at anyone building reports who wants them to feel less like a spreadsheet and more like a polished dashboard.

steps.heading: Prepare an image column in your data
steps.body: Your dataset needs a column containing image URLs or paths, since Power BI displays images by referencing a link rather than embedding a file directly.

steps.heading: Mark the column as an image data category
steps.body: Select the column in the Data view and set its Data Category to Image URL so Power BI knows to render it visually instead of as text.

steps.heading: Add the field to a table or card visual
steps.body: Drag the image column into a table, card, or similar visual, and Power BI will automatically display the picture instead of the raw URL.

steps.heading: Adjust image size and layout
steps.body: Use the visual's formatting options to control image height and row spacing so pictures stay proportional and the table remains readable.

faq.question: Can I upload images directly into Power BI instead of using a URL?
faq.answer: Power BI's built-in image support relies on URLs, so images generally need to be hosted somewhere accessible rather than embedded as files.

faq.question: Why is my image showing as text instead of a picture?
faq.answer: This usually means the column's Data Category hasn't been set to Image URL yet, which is required for Power BI to render it as an image.

faq.question: Do images slow down report performance?
faq.answer: Large numbers of high-resolution images can affect load times, so it's worth using appropriately sized images, especially in tables with many rows.

faq.question: Can I use images in cards as well as tables?
faq.answer: Yes, the same image data category works across cards and several other visual types that accept image fields.
